selected_important_turn.text: i will give you that in exchange for 1 food and 3 water with 2 firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the agent's first concrete counter-offer and it substantially shapes the distributional frame of the negotiation — it concedes the opponent's demand for extra food but extracts almost all remaining resources, locking the bargaining into a zero-sum stance.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The offer is highly greedy and unilateral, misses opportunities to trade on different item values, gives no rationale or options, and escalates competition rather than opening integrative possibilities. It risks rejection and harms the chance of a high-quality joint agreement.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the demand and shift toward value-creating trades: propose a smaller claim on water/firewood, ask which items the opponent values less, offer multiple package options (e.g., take the extra food plus one water, or accept two water if you get one extra food), and provide a brief justification to make the trade seem fairer.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — opening trade options and reducing greed enables more mutually valuable splits.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — a less lopsided offer will be perceived as fairer.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a softer, option-rich proposal reduces likelihood of outright rejection and speeds agreement.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — appearing willing to compromise and explain reduces hostility and builds trust.
